description: Forcing authenticated users to perform unintended actions without their consent.

complexity_factors.question: Was the forged request sent using an admin or privileged session?
complexity_factors.reason: A forged request in an admin session can make account-level changes that are hard to reverse, increasing containment and recovery effort.
complexity_factors.source: MITRE T1078 — Valid Accounts: abuse of privileged sessions increases impact and containment complexity

complexity_factors.question: Did the forged request perform a state-changing action such as password change or fund transfer?
complexity_factors.reason: State changes caused by CSRF may be difficult to reverse and require additional recovery steps beyond simply blocking the request.
complexity_factors.source: OWASP A01:2021 — Broken Access Control: unauthorized state changes are rated high impact

complexity_factors.question: Was there no CSRF token in place to prevent the forged request?
complexity_factors.reason: Absence of CSRF tokens means the application had no built-in defense, making detection harder since the request appeared legitimate.
complexity_factors.source: OWASP CSRF Prevention Cheat Sheet — missing CSRF tokens reduce detectability of forged requests

complexity_factors.question: Did the attacker send repeated forged requests across multiple sessions?
complexity_factors.reason: Multiple forged requests mean the blue team must identify and reverse all affected sessions, not just one.
complexity_factors.source: NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 — Section 3.2: Scope assessment includes identifying all affected accounts and sessions

ttc_ranges.source: NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 — Section 3.3: Low severity incidents should be contained rapidly with minimal escalation
NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 — Section 3.3: Medium severity incidents require analysis before containment
NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 — Section 3.3: High severity incidents involve multiple IR phases before containment is possible

rules.description: Alert must be investigated within 10 minutes of being raised
rules.trigger: No alert_investigation_started event within 600 seconds of alert_raised
rules.source: NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 — Section 3.2.4: Initial analysis should begin promptly after detection

rules.description: Incident must be confirmed — anomalous authenticated request must be escalated
rules.trigger: No incident_confirmed event in event log
rules.source: NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 — Section 3.2: Incident declaration is a required step in the IR lifecycle

rules.description: Containment must be initiated before max_tolerance threshold
rules.trigger: No containment_initiated event before max_tolerance_min seconds
rules.source: NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 — Section 3.3: Containment strategy must be selected and implemented promptly

rules.description: Containment must succeed — session invalidated and forged action reversed
rules.source: NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 — Section 3.3: Containment must be confirmed before incident can be closed

scenarios.label: CSRF on standard user — good response
scenarios.description: Blue team detects anomalous authenticated request, confirms forged action, and invalidates session within acceptable time.

logs.detail: Wazuh rule 31170 fired — anomalous authenticated POST request detected without CSRF token
Analyst-1 opened alert and reviewed session logs
Analyst-2 confirmed forged password change request from external origin
Analyst-3 initiated session invalidation and account lock

scenarios.label: CSRF on admin session — missed entirely
scenarios.description: Blue team fails to investigate the alert. No incident confirmed, no containment. Auto-fail triggered.

logs.detail: Wazuh rule 31171 fired — forged request in admin session detected
Analyst-1 incorrectly dismissed as normal admin activity
